records.ety: [OE. ordeinen, OF. ordener, F. ordonner, fr. L. ordinare, from ordo, ordinis, order. See Order, and cf. Ordinance.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Ordained (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Ordaining.]

defs: To set in order; to arrange according to rule; to regulate; to set; to establish.
quotes: The stake that shall be ordained on either side.

defs: To regulate, or establish, by appointment, decree, or law; to constitute; to decree; to appoint; to institute.
quotes: Jeroboam ordained a feast in the eighth month.
And doth the power that man adores ordain Their doom ?

defs: To set apart for an office; to appoint.
To invest with ministerial or sacerdotal functions; to introduce into the office of the Christian ministry, by the laying on of hands, or other forms; to set apart by the ceremony of ordination.
quotes: Meletius was ordained by Arian bishops.
